2604.09687.title: Grid2Matrix: Revealing Digital Agnosia in Vision-Language Models
2604.09687.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel on many multimodal reasoning benchmarks, but these evaluations often do not require an exhaustive readout of the image and can therefore obscure failures in faithfully capturing all visual details. We introduce Grid2Matrix (G2M), a controlled benchmark in which a model is shown a color grid and a color-to-number mapping, and must output the corresponding matrix. By varying grid size and the number of colors, G2M provides a simple way to increase visual complexity while minimizing semantic confounds. We find that VLMs exhibit a sharp early collapse in zero-shot end-to-end evaluation, failing on surprisingly small grids rather than degrading gradually as the task becomes denser. We probe the visual encoders of VLMs from two representative families and find that they preserve substantially more of the grid information than the corresponding end-to-end outputs. This suggests that the failure is not explained by visual encoding alone, but also reflects a gap between what remains recoverable from visual features and what is ultimately expressed in language. We term this gap \textit{Digital Agnosia}. Further analyses show that these errors are highly structured and depend strongly on how grid cells overlap with visual patch boundaries. We also find that common strategies such as model scaling and multimodal alignment do not fully eliminate this failure mode. We expect G2M to serve as a useful testbed for understanding where and how VLMs lose fine visual details, and for evaluating tasks where missing even small visual details can matter, such as tables, charts, forms, and GUIs.
2604.09687.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在許多多模態推理基準上表現優異，但這些評估通常不需要對圖像進行徹底的讀取，因此可能會掩蓋在忠實捕捉所有視覺細節方面的失敗。我們介紹了 Grid2Matrix（G2M），這是一個受控基準，其中模型被展示一個顏色網格和顏色到數字的映射，並必須輸出相應的矩陣。通過變更網格大小和顏色數量，G2M 提供了一種簡單的方法來增加視覺複雜性，同時最小化語義混淆。我們發現 VLMs 在零-shot 端到端評估中出現了明顯的早期崩潰，對意外小的網格失敗，而不是隨著任務變得更密集而逐漸降級。我們探查了來自兩個代表性家族的 VLMs 的視覺編碼器，發現它們保留了比相應的端到端輸出更多的網格信息。這表明，失敗並不僅僅由視覺編碼解釋，還反映了從視覺特徵中恢復的內容與最終在語言中表達的內容之間的差距。我們將這一差距稱為 \textit{數位失認症}。進一步的分析顯示，這些錯誤高度結構化，並且強烈依賴於網格單元如何與視覺補丁邊界重疊。我們還發現，常見策略如模型縮放和多模態對齊並未完全消除這種失敗模式。我們預期 G2M 將作為一個有用的測試平台，以理解 VLMs 在何處以及如何失去細微的視覺細節，以及評估在缺失即使是小的視覺細節也可能影響的任務，如表格、圖表、表單和 GUI。
2604.09687.author: Yunkai Zhang et.al.
2604.09687.authors: Yunkai Zhang, Linda Li, Yingxin Cui, Xiyuan Ruan, Zeyu Zheng, Kezhen Chen, Yi Zhang, Diji Yang
